case_name: Estate of Susan J. Moulder v. Park, M.D.

summary.brief: Estate of Susan J. Moulder v. Park, M.D.. Medical malpractice case involving obstetrics gynaecology.

evidence_index.text: The plaintiff alleges the defendant doctor violated the applicable standard of care by failing to timely review and notify the plaintiff’s late wife of a CT chest report that identified new pulmonary nodules in her lungs.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Mrs. Moulder was diagnosed with Stage II squamous cell carcinoma of the mouth in September 2016.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Mrs. Moulder underwent a chest CT on January 12, 2018, as part of the clinical trial protocol.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The radiologist’s impression noted 'a 7 x 6 mm nodule in the posterior aspect of the right middle lobe along the major fissure.'
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Park maintains she reviewed the report promptly and recognized the recommendation for a three-month follow-up but made the decision not to tell Mrs. Moulder immediately.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: On July 27, 2018, Mrs. Moulder underwent the recommended chest CT, which revealed the nodule had grown to 10 x 19 mm.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: On December 1, 2018, Mrs. Moulder died from end stage liver disease from alcoholism and lung mass with metastatic disease to liver.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Harris stated that he 'believed Mrs. Moulder’s mental condition would have been a lot better if Dr. Park had not been negligent.'
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Harris

evidence_index.text: The Court grants Dr. Park’s motion for summary judgment as to all Mr. Moulder’s claims.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

patient_demographics.relevant_social_history: Significant alcohol intake and smoking history

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Mrs. Moulder was diagnosed with Stage II squamous cell carcinoma of the mouth in September 2016. She underwent surgical resection and was enrolled in a clinical trial for post-operative radiation therapy. A chest CT on January 12, 2018, noted a 7 x 6 mm nodule in the right middle lobe and a 5 mm left apical nodule.
past_medical_history: Stage II squamous cell carcinoma of the mouth

focused_exam.system: Examination mentioned but findings not specified

initial_state.initial_working_problem: Follow-up for cancer treatment and monitoring of pulmonary nodules

requestables.name: Chest CT
requestables.description: CT scan of the chest to monitor pulmonary nodules

reveal.result_summary: 7 x 6 mm nodule in the right middle lobe, 5 mm left apical nodule
reveal.clinical_significance: Potential for metastatic disease or primary lung cancer
reveal.decision_impact: Recommendation for follow-up imaging in three months

requestables.name: Follow-up Chest CT
requestables.description: Follow-up CT scan to assess growth of pulmonary nodules

reveal.decision_impact: Prompted referral for further diagnostic workup

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents for follow-up as part of cancer treatment protocol

timeline_phases.name: Follow-up and Outcome
timeline_phases.description: Patient returns for follow-up and further imaging

decision_points.clinical_context: Review of January 2018 chest CT findings
decision_points.prompt: What should be the follow-up plan for the pulmonary nodules identified on the CT scan?

options.description: Order a follow-up CT scan in three months as recommended by the radiologist
options.clinical_reasoning: Timely follow-up to monitor potential growth of nodules

options.description: Proceed with a six-month follow-up scan
options.clinical_reasoning: Decision based on minimizing patient anxiety without immediate therapeutic benefit

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant chose to proceed with a six-month follow-up scan

expected_action_court.description: A three-month follow-up CT should have been ordered

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to follow radiologist's recommendation for timely follow-up
explanation.what_should_have_happened: A three-month follow-up CT should have been ordered to monitor nodule growth
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care as established by expert testimony

patient_outcome.description: Patient died from end stage liver disease and lung mass with metastatic disease to liver

key_findings: No proximate cause between alleged negligence and patient's death
No substantial physical symptoms of mental anguish proven

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to order timely follow-up CT
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Not applicable as no liability was found

tests_performed.result: 7 x 6 mm nodule in the right middle lobe, 5 mm left apical nodule

allegations.allegation: Failure to timely notify and follow-up on pulmonary nodules

court_findings.finding: No proximate cause between alleged negligence and patient's death
court_findings.legal_significance: No liability for mental anguish claim
